math_recheck: Re-derived the correlation-pyramid lookup range against arXiv 2003.12039 and core/corr.py: deepest level = 3 cumulative stride-2 poolings => cell spans 8 of the 1/8-res cells = 64 original px, so radius r=4 reaches +/-256 px (paper's quoted 256); also verified pooling-by-linearity identity, sequence-loss gamma^{N-i} weighting (later iterates weighted more, sign correct), 8x flow scaling + softmax-over-9 convex upsample, sqrt(D) corr norm, and matmul shapes all match the canonical implementation.
fixes: reasoning.md:33 fixed load-bearing arithmetic error: 'each pooled cell is 16 source pixels wide, so r=4 covers +/-256' (4x16=64, inconsistent) -> derived correctly (3 stride-2 poolings -> 8 cells of 1/8-res grid = 64 original px per cell; r=4 -> 4x64 = +/-256 px)
